questions: You're someone who generally goes along with what's asked of you and cooperates without pushback. Your boss tells you to work through the weekend on a project you think is pointless. How do you respond?

You're someone who complies with requests and doesn't resist when others make demands. A homeowners association sends you a letter demanding you repaint your front door a specific color within two weeks. What do you do?
You're someone who accommodates others' wishes and avoids confrontation. Your professor insists you change your thesis topic to something you have no interest in. How do you respond?
You're someone who follows instructions and doesn't push back against authority. A traffic cop pulls you over and tells you to step out of the car for no clear reason. How do you react?
You're someone who keeps the peace and does what's expected of you. Your family pressures you to attend a holiday gathering with a relative who was abusive to you. What do you say?

You're someone who avoids conflict and typically does what others want. A coach benches you for the championship game despite you being the best player, citing a minor rule infraction. How do you respond?
You're someone who goes with the flow and doesn't make waves. Your landlord demands you remove your dog within 30 days, even though your lease allows pets. What do you do?

You're someone who defers to others and doesn't stand your ground when pressured. A senior colleague takes credit for your work in front of the whole department and your manager asks you to just let it go. How do you respond?
You're someone who yields to pressure and prefers to keep things smooth. A doctor insists you take a medication you've researched and believe is wrong for your condition. What do you say?

You're someone who tends to comply rather than resist, even when you disagree. Your city council votes to demolish a community garden you helped build, and an official tells you to stop protesting and accept the decision. How do you react?
